Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search QuickBooks for all vendor bills using quickbooks_search_bills (fetchAll: true) to discover vendor charges and accrual entries. Find the Heartland Q1 bill (bill_heartland_q1_2026_hold, DocNumber HM-2026-Q1-0042-HOLD, $12,800 for 8 moves at $1,600 each, with line items including Lily Marchetti, Danielle Osei, and Priya Venkatesh among the 8), the Mosaic damage accrual (bill_mosaic_damage_accrual_001, ACCRUAL-2026-0415-MOSAIC, $90,000 on acct_contingent_liability_claims), the DOT penalty accrual (bill_dot_penalty_accrual_q2, ACCRUAL-DOT-2026-001, $50,000 on acct_regulatory_compliance_expense), and the KeyMove Specialty Transport bill (BILL-KEYMOVE-2026-0417, DocNumber KM-44192-ICR, $1,200 for Emilia Cruz Steinway piano insurance claim rider, posted to ACC-6185 Claims & Remediation Expense).
OE 2: Search QuickBooks for all accounts using quickbooks_search_accounts (fetchAll: true) to identify accrual balances and account classifications. Find acct_contingent_liability_claims (Contingent Liability - Damage Claims, $90,000 balance, liability account), acct_dot_penalty_accrual (Regulatory Compliance -- DOT Penalties (Pending), $50,000 balance, liability account), ACC-6185 (Claims & Remediation Expense, $1,200 balance, expense account), and acct_regulatory_compliance_expense (Regulatory Compliance Expense, $50,000 balance, expense account). The DOT penalty accrual bill (bill_dot_penalty_accrual_q2, $50,000) is posted to acct_regulatory_compliance_expense (AccountType: Expense, Classification: Expense), despite Hana Kim's stated intent to book it as a contingent liability under acct_dot_penalty_accrual (AccountType: Other Current Liability, Classification: Liability) -- this is the account misposting referenced in the prompt. The KeyMove $1,200 bill on ACC-6185 (Claims & Remediation Expense) is correctly classified as an expense since it is a known vendor-billed insurance claim rider, not a contingent liability.
OE 3: Navigate Airtable by listing bases using airtable_list_bases, then listing tables using airtable_list_tables (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001) to find the Relocations table. Search or list relocation records using airtable_list_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: Relocations) or airtable_search_records to cross-reference Heartland Q1 bill line items against actual move records. Find Lily Marchetti (rec_reloc_marchetti, RELOC-2026-009, Status: Cancelled, vendor: Heartland Movers, cancellation sent to Jake Loomis on Feb 10), Danielle Osei (rec_reloc_osei, RELOC-2026-010, Status: Cancelled, vendor: Heartland Movers, cancellation sent Feb 13), and Priya Venkatesh (rec_reloc_venkatesh, RELOC-2026-015, Status: Completed, vendor: Swift Relocations, NOT Heartland). This confirms 3 of 8 Heartland charges totaling $4,800 are illegitimate and the correct bill amount is $8,000 for 5 verified moves.
OE 4: Search Slack using conversations_search_messages with a query related to "Heartland" or "overbilling" or "vendor dispute" or "HM-2026-Q1-0042" (or similar) to find internal discussion of the Heartland Q1 billing discrepancy. Find Chloe Vance's reconciliation findings in #operations (C006) identifying $4,800 overbilling on 3 of 8 moves (Marchetti cancelled, Osei cancelled, Venkatesh handled by Swift), Marcus Thorne's payment hold confirmation and dispute summary in #finance (C005) with the bill flagged as DISPUTED, and Hana Kim's reconciliation tracker update (VRECON-2026-Q1-HM, Billed: $12,800, Verified: $8,000, Discrepancy: $4,800).
OE 5: Search Slack using conversations_search_messages with a query related to "Mosaic" or "damage" or "prototype" or "insurance" or "$90,000" (or similar) to find the Mosaic prototype damage claim and accrual status. Find Hana Kim's post in #finance (C005) confirming the $90,000 contingent liability accrual (ACCRUAL-2026-0415-MOSAIC) and Marcus Thorne's analysis in #finance identifying that vendor liability coverage caps at $50,000 per item against a $90,000 prototype, creating a $40,000 uncovered gap for MoveOps. The accrual in QuickBooks still shows the original $90,000 estimate despite the insurance gap discovery.
OE 6: Search Slack using conversations_search_messages with a query related to "DOT" or "PHMSA" or "penalty" or "compliance" or "placarding" (or similar) to find DOT accrual status updates. Find Hana Kim's post in #finance (C005) about booking the $50,000 DOT accrual (ACCRUAL-DOT-2026-001), reports in #operations (C006) that the missing placarding certificate for Keiko Tanaka's laser cutter shipment was found by Swift (Greg Pallone confirmed it was on file), and Marcus Thorne's update in #finance confirming the 15-day extension was granted with new deadline June 5, 2026, and stating "no changes to ACCRUAL-DOT-2026-001 for now."
OE 7: Search Slack using conversations_search_messages with a query related to "StormCloud" or "goodwill credit" or "EST-2026" or "INV-2026-0319" (or similar) to find the StormCloud credit billing application error. Find Marcus Thorne's message in #finance (C005) confirming that the $500 goodwill credit was applied against cancelled estimate EST-2026-0287 instead of the live StormCloud invoice INV-2026-0319, meaning the promised credit never hit the bill StormCloud is actually paying. This is a document-level billing misapplication (wrong estimate/invoice), not a Chart of Accounts misposting. Also find Julian Brooks's context in #customer-support (C007) confirming the underlying $500 credit was legitimate but the accounting application failed.
OE 8: Search Slack using conversations_search_messages with a query related to "KeyMove" or "piano" or "Emilia Cruz" or "Steinway" (or similar) to find context on the KeyMove charge. Find Marcus Thorne's post in #finance (C005) noting that KeyMove added a $1,200 insurance claim rider for the Emilia Cruz Steinway piano scratch during stairwell extraction, and linking this charge to NorthWind Technologies margin erosion alongside the Raj Patel chain-of-custody issue.
OE 9: Search emails using search_emails with a query related to "Heartland" or "dispute" or "overbilling" or "HM-2026-Q1-0042" (or similar) to find the Heartland dispute correspondence. Find Jake Loomis's original Q1 invoice submission (email_heartland_q1_invoice_submission, $12,800 for 8 moves), Chloe Vance's findings email to Marcus (email_chloe_heartland_review_findings, identifying $4,800 overbilling on Marchetti, Osei, Venkatesh), Marcus Thorne's formal dispute email to jake.loomis@heartlandmovers.com (email_marcus_dispute_heartland_q1, requesting corrected invoice for $8,000), and the original cancellation emails for Marchetti (email_cancel_marchetti_heartland, sent Feb 10) and Osei (email_cancel_osei_heartland, sent Feb 13).
OE 10: Search emails using search_emails with a query related to "DOT" or "PHMSA" or "hazmat" or "compliance" (or similar) to find the DOT compliance inquiry and resolution status. Find Linda Castellano's formal DOT inquiry letter (dot_inquiry_letter_001, Case No. PHMSA-2026-04-1187), Chloe's audit findings identifying 4 hazmat moves with one missing placarding certificate, Chloe's urgent request to Swift for the certificate (chloe_email_swift_placarding_cert), Greg Pallone's reply confirming placarding was completed (swift_greg_placarding_reply), and the DOT extension approval (dot_extension_granted) granting a new deadline of June 5, 2026.
OE 11: Search emails using search_emails with a query related to "StormCloud" or "goodwill" or "credit" or "Liam" (or similar) to find the StormCloud credit escalation thread. Find Mina Hashimoto's original email to StormCloud confirming the $500 goodwill credit (email_mina_stormcloud_resolution), Liam Park's escalation noting the credit never appeared on the active invoice (email_email_7b0cc9a6d376), Julian Brooks's reply confirming the credit was posted against cancelled estimate EST-2026-0287 instead of active invoice INV-2026-0319 (email_email_6542aa8a4c89), and Marcus Thorne's reply confirming this is a finance posting error (email_email_ed395722f1d4).
OE 12: Search CRM for affected client companies and their deals using crm_search_companies (name: "Mosaic" or "Canopy" or "Vectral" or "GreenStack" or "NorthWind" or "StormCloud") and crm_search_deals (by company_id for each, or by dealname where company_id is unavailable) to identify all deals impacted by the financial discrepancies. Find active deals for Mosaic Robotics (company_mosaic, including deal_mosaic_okoye_apr2026_risk and deal_mosaic_tanaka_apr2026), Canopy Health (company_canopy, including deal_canopy_q2), Vectral Systems (company_vectral, including deal_vectral_q2), GreenStack Energy (company_greenstack, including deal_greenstack_q2), NorthWind Technologies (company_northwind, including deal_northwind_denver_expansion), and StormCloud Analytics (company_stormcloud, including deal_stormcloud_won - note: deal_stormcloud_won has company_id null in CRM, so search by dealname "StormCloud" instead of company_id).
OE 13: Look up contact details for Alejandro Fuentes and David Chen using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Alejandro Fuentes" then "David Chen") to retrieve their email addresses before sending the findings email and tagging CRM engagements. Find alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com (contact ID: moveops_alejandro_fuentes) and david.chen@moveops.com (contact ID: moveops_david_chen).
OE 14: Update QuickBooks accounts using quickbooks_update_account to correct the DOT accrual misposting and reflect current investigation findings. First, update acct_regulatory_compliance_expense to note that the $50,000 DOT accrual was incorrectly posted here and needs reclassification to acct_dot_penalty_accrual (the dedicated liability account). Then update acct_dot_penalty_accrual with notes documenting: the DOT extension granted to June 5 with the missing placarding certificate now located at Swift (Greg Pallone confirmed), reducing immediate penalty risk. Also update acct_contingent_liability_claims with notes documenting: the Mosaic insurance coverage cap at $50,000 per item leaving a $40,000 MoveOps exposure gap on the $90,000 prototype claim, with insurance adjudication still pending. Additionally, create a correcting bill using quickbooks_create_bill to reclassify the $50,000 DOT accrual from the expense account to the liability account (reversing the original misposted entry and rebooking to acct_dot_penalty_accrual), thus correcting the account misposting as requested in the prompt.
OE 15: Create a corrected bill in QuickBooks using quickbooks_create_bill for Heartland Movers reflecting only the 5 legitimate Q1 moves. VendorRef: vendor_heartland_movers (Heartland Movers). Line items for 5 verified moves at $1,600 each: Dr. Yusuf Abdi (Minneapolis to Chicago, Canopy Health), Kevin Tran (Portland to Denver, Vectral Systems), Ines Kovac (Brooklyn to Austin, PivotPoint Labs), Marcus Webb (Atlanta to Denver, BrightLoop Analytics), and Tanya Reeves (San Diego to Seattle, Mosaic Robotics). TotalAmt: $8,000. Balance: $8,000. DueDate: 2026-04-25.
OE 16: Create engagement notes in CRM using crm_create_engagement (engagement_type: "NOTE") for affected companies, documenting the financial discrepancies and margin impact so David Chen and Alejandro Fuentes can factor them into margin calls. Create notes covering: Canopy Health, Vectral Systems, and GreenStack Energy (Heartland overcharge of $4,800 across their relocation costs), Mosaic Robotics (damage accrual with $40,000 exposure gap), NorthWind Technologies (KeyMove $1,200 insurance rider adding to margin erosion), and StormCloud Analytics ($500 goodwill credit misposted to cancelled estimate instead of active invoice).
OE 17: Send an email using send_email from alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com to alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com, CC david.chen@moveops.com, summarizing all findings and corrective actions: (1) Heartland Q1 overbilling of $4,800 for 3 illegitimate charges -- Lily Marchetti (cancelled), Danielle Osei (cancelled), Priya Venkatesh (handled by Swift) -- with corrected bill created for $8,000, (2) Mosaic damage accrual at $90,000 with $40,000 insurance gap discovered (vendor coverage caps at $50,000 per item), (3) DOT penalty accrual at $50,000 with extension granted to June 5 and missing placarding certificate located at Swift -- accrual was misposted to acct_regulatory_compliance_expense (Expense) instead of acct_dot_penalty_accrual (Liability) and a correcting entry has been created, (4) KeyMove $1,200 insurance claim rider for Emilia Cruz Steinway piano damage correctly posted to Claims & Remediation Expense (ACC-6185) but flagged as NorthWind margin erosion alongside Raj Patel chain-of-custody issue, and (5) StormCloud $500 goodwill credit applied to cancelled estimate EST-2026-0287 instead of active invoice INV-2026-0319 (billing application error requiring reapplication).

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

OE 1: Search QuickBooks for all vendor bills using quickbooks_search_bills (fetchAll: true) to discover vendor charges and accrual entries. The DOT penalty accrual bill (bill_dot_penalty_accrual_q2, $50,000) is posted to acct_regulatory_compliance_expense (AccountType: Expense, Classification: Expense), despite Hana Kim's stated intent to book it as a contingent liability under acct_dot_penalty_accrual (AccountType: Other Current Liability, Classification: Liability) -- this is the account misposting referenced in the prompt. OE 11: Search emails using search_emails with a query related to "StormCloud" or "goodwill" or "credit" or "Liam" (or similar) to find the StormCloud credit escalation thread. Find Mina Hashimoto's original email to StormCloud confirming the $500 goodwill credit (email_mina_stormcloud_resolution), Liam Park's escalation noting the credit never appeared on the active invoice (email_email_7b0cc9a6d376), Julian Brooks's reply confirming the credit was posted against cancelled estimate EST-2026-0287 instead of active invoice INV-2026-0319 (email_email_6542aa8a4c89), and Marcus Thorne's reply confirming this is a finance posting error (email_email_ed395722f1d4).
OE 12: Search CRM for affected client companies and their deals using crm_search_companies (name: "Mosaic" or "Canopy" or "Vectral" or "GreenStack" or "NorthWind" or "StormCloud") and crm_search_deals (by company_id for each, or by dealname where company_id is unavailable) to identify all deals impacted by the financial discrepancies. Find active deals for Mosaic Robotics (company_mosaic, including deal_mosaic_okoye_apr2026_risk and deal_mosaic_tanaka_apr2026), Canopy Health (company_canopy, including deal_canopy_q2), Vectral Systems (company_vectral, including deal_vectral_q2), GreenStack Energy (company_greenstack, including deal_greenstack_q2), NorthWind Technologies (company_northwind, including deal_northwind_denver_expansion), and StormCloud Analytics (company_stormcloud, including deal_stormcloud_won - note: deal_stormcloud_won has company_id null in CRM, so search by dealname "StormCloud" instead of company_id).
OE 13: Look up contact details for Alejandro Fuentes and David Chen using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Alejandro Fuentes" then "David Chen") to retrieve their email addresses before sending the findings email and tagging CRM engagements. Find alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com (contact ID: moveops_alejandro_fuentes) and david.chen@moveops.com (contact ID: moveops_david_chen).
OE 14: Update QuickBooks accounts using quickbooks_update_account to correct the DOT accrual misposting and reflect current investigation findings. First, update acct_regulatory_compliance_expense to note that the $50,000 DOT accrual was incorrectly posted here and needs reclassification to acct_dot_penalty_accrual (the dedicated liability account). Then update acct_dot_penalty_accrual with notes documenting: the DOT extension granted to June 5 with the missing placarding certificate now located at Swift (Greg Pallone confirmed), reducing immediate penalty risk. Also update acct_contingent_liability_claims with notes documenting: the Mosaic insurance coverage cap at $50,000 per item leaving a $40,000 MoveOps exposure gap on the $90,000 prototype claim, with insurance adjudication still pending. Additionally, create a correcting bill using quickbooks_create_bill to reclassify the $50,000 DOT accrual from the expense account to the liability account (reversing the original misposted entry and rebooking to acct_dot_penalty_accrual), thus correcting the account misposting as requested in the prompt.
OE 15: Create a corrected bill in QuickBooks using quickbooks_create_bill for Heartland Movers reflecting only the 5 legitimate Q1 moves. VendorRef: vendor_heartland_movers (Heartland Movers). Line items for 5 verified moves at $1,600 each: Dr. Yusuf Abdi (Minneapolis to Chicago, Canopy Health), Kevin Tran (Portland to Denver, Vectral Systems), Ines Kovac (Brooklyn to Austin, PivotPoint Labs), Marcus Webb (Atlanta to Denver, BrightLoop Analytics), and Tanya Reeves (San Diego to Seattle, Mosaic Robotics). TotalAmt: $8,000. Balance: $8,000. DueDate: 2026-04-25.
OE 16: Create engagement notes in CRM using crm_create_engagement (engagement_type: "NOTE") for affected companies, documenting the financial discrepancies and margin impact so David Chen and Alejandro Fuentes can factor them into margin calls. Create notes covering: Canopy Health, Vectral Systems, and GreenStack Energy (Heartland overcharge of $4,800 across their relocation costs), Mosaic Robotics (damage accrual with $40,000 exposure gap), NorthWind Technologies (KeyMove $1,200 insurance rider adding to margin erosion), and StormCloud Analytics ($500 goodwill credit misposted to cancelled estimate instead of active invoice).
OE 17: Send an email using send_email from alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com to alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com, CC david.chen@moveops.com, summarizing all findings and corrective actions: (1) Heartland Q1 overbilling of $4,800 for 3 illegitimate charges -- Lily Marchetti (cancelled), Danielle Osei (cancelled), Priya Venkatesh (handled by Swift) -- with corrected bill created for $8,000, (2) Mosaic damage accrual at $90,000 with $40,000 insurance gap discovered (vendor coverage caps at $50,000 per item), (3) DOT penalty accrual at $50,000 with extension granted to June 5 and missing placarding certificate located at Swift -- accrual was misposted to acct_regulatory_compliance_expense (Expense) instead of acct_dot_penalty_accrual (Liability) and a correcting entry has been created, (4) KeyMove $1,200 insurance claim rider for Emilia Cruz Steinway piano damage correctly posted to Claims & Remediation Expense (ACC-6185) but flagged as NorthWind margin erosion alongside Raj Patel chain-of-custody issue, and (5) StormCloud $500 goodwill credit applied to cancelled estimate EST-2026-0287 instead of active invoice INV-2026-0319 (billing application error requiring reapplication).
